block_01: As you already know, the promotion of IQ Option to users from the
block_05: based on the CySEC’s requirements. All traffic sources used to attract users from EEA countries have to be pre-approved and are regularly checked to ensure full compliance with the guidelines.
block_06: The recent assessment of your EEA traffic sources has shown that they either do not meet the requirements or are unavailable. Unfortunately, this means that we had to suspend your verification for EEA countries. Meanwhile, your affiliate links will keep working in non-EEA countries as usual. As soon as you amend the traffic sources, send them to your manager for re-approval. If they are found to be in compliance with the regulator’s requirements, the verification for EEA countries will be reinstated. Please note that if you do not target the EEA countries at this time, no actions are required.
block_07: To help you understand what to look at first, we compiled a list of the most common issues:
block_08: Advertising trading instruments or features that are not available in EEA countries.
block_09: Make sure to determine the audience you plan to target with each of your traffic sources. If the material is intended for traders from EEA countries, it has to reflect the correct trading conditions available to them. Namely, do not promote binary options, higher leverages, trading tournaments, or VIP status in such materials. Be advised that using the materials with mixed information that targets both EEA and non-EEA audience is to be avoided.
block_10: Missing, incorrect, or not prominent risk warnings.
block_11: All promotions and invitations to open an account (registration links) must include a relevant risk warning that should be written in bold, in a text not smaller than the regular text of the material, in the language of the material, and in a place that would attract a reader’s eye to them. Risk warning texts can be found in the
block_13: Using misleading statements or focusing on the benefits of a financial product or a service only.
block_14: A balance between a potential benefit and the relevant risk has to be maintained. Claims like “trading is easy/exciting”, “suitable for everyone/beginners”, “start winning/making/earning money”, “trading leads to success, a change in lifestyle, financial freedom” or any other claims with the similar promotional meaning must not be used.
block_15: Advertising IQ Option using traffic sources dedicated to certain topics.
block_16: Traffic sources that exclusively offer trading signals, social trading, copy trading, automated trading, robots, investment advice, portfolio management services, as well as gaming or gambling, cannot be used for the promotion in EEA countries.
block_17: Using outdated content and promo materials.
block_18: Most of the materials published a few years ago no longer reflect the current trading conditions and have to be updated. As for the promo materials, please make sure to use the up-to-date ones available in the
block_20: Once you make appropriate amendments, send your manager a message with a list of the traffic sources you would like to verify for EEA countries. Please be advised that it may take a few days to review them.
